lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Describing People & Places
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tânia Rosa | Aula 15 -- Describing People & Places

listenings.text: Let me describe a person I met on my last trip. He was a tall, friendly man with short curly hair. He worked at a small cafe near my hotel. Every morning he was smiling and talking to everyone. He always wore a big blue hat. One day I asked him for directions, and he walked with me for ten minutes, just to be kind. He was not a famous person, but he made my trip special. When I think of that city, I always remember his warm smile. A friendly face can change a whole journey.

I want to describe my favorite place in the world. It is a small, ancient town near the sea. The streets are narrow and quiet, and the old stone houses are beautiful. In the morning the main square is peaceful, but in the evening it becomes crowded and full of music. There is a lovely rooftop cafe with a stunning view of the water. I love to sit there and watch the sunset. It is a cozy, charming place, and every corner tells a story. I cannot wait to go back.

extra_audio.text: First, Tânia arrived in a small, ancient town by the sea. Then she walked through the narrow, quiet streets and looked at the old stone houses. After that, she met a tall, friendly man with short curly hair. Next, he showed her a cozy rooftop cafe with a stunning view. Finally, she watched the sunset and decided it was her favorite place in the world.
